title: Piquant Molten Norwegian Trout Roast
description: A fragrant Norwegian trout roast with a molten center, brushed with a honey-lemon butter glaze and aromatic dill.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Pat the trout fillets dry, then rub each side with sea salt, black pepper, and a sprinkle of chopped fresh dill.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter over low heat and stir in the lemon juice, white wine, and honey until glossy.
Brush the butter mixture over each trout fillet, making sure the center is lightly coated.
Arrange the fillets on the prepared sheet, skin side down, and place in the preheated oven for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, flip the fillets and brush again, then roast for an additional 5 minutes until the edges are golden and the center is just starting to set.
Remove the trout from the oven and let it rest for 2 minutes so the molten center retains its texture.
Garnish with extra dill and a splash of lemon juice before serving on a warm plate.
Pair with a side of lightly sautéed spinach and a drizzle of extra butter if desired.
